cards.f: Ran continuously at Maratha Mandir for over 25 years. The train that never left.
cards.c: "Boy meets girl. Father says no. They go to Europe anyway. Entire Punjab gives permission eventually."

cards.f: Highest-grossing Bollywood film of 2001. The reason every NRI cries somewhere over the Atlantic.
cards.c: "Eldest son marries a poor girl. Family is devastated. They fly to London to express this devastation at length."

cards.f: SRK played a terminally ill man who meddles in everyone's relationships. Nobody stopped him.
cards.c: "Best friend is in love with your wife. He is also dying. He decides to help arrange the wedding anyway."

cards.f: Kamal Haasan wrote, directed, and starred. A remake of Mrs Doubtfire but considerably more unhinged.
cards.c: "Man disguises as a woman to be his own child's nanny. Sari. Discovery. Redemption. More sari."

cards.f: Oscar-nominated. Aamir Khan spent a full year actually learning cricket.
cards.c: "Village challenges the British Empire to a cricket match to avoid paying taxes. They win. Obviously."

cards.f: Flopped in 2004. Now considered SRK's finest. The ultimate sleeper film.
cards.c: "NASA engineer returns to his village. Is horrified. Fixes the water supply. Stays. NASA says nothing."

cards.f: SRK waived his fee for this. Said it was the best script he'd read in years.
cards.c: "Women's hockey team hates their coach. Coach has a secret. They win the World Cup. Everyone cries."

cards.f: A dog named Tuffy had more screen time than half the cast. He earned it.
cards.c: "An entire family attends two weddings across four hours. A dog has a pivotal role in the resolution."

cards.f: Highest-grossing Bollywood film of 2013 at the time. Shot across six states.
cards.c: "Man boards a train to scatter his grandfather's ashes. Accidentally ends up in a Tamil gangster's village. Accidentally gets engaged."

cards.f: Aamir Khan played a 20-year-old at age 44. Convincingly.
cards.c: "Three college idiots. One is secretly a genius. This is discovered ten years later at a goat farm."

cards.f: Based loosely on a true story. The real girl was found and returned to Pakistan in 2015.
cards.c: "Extremely honest man tries to return a lost child across a border that does not want to be crossed. Becomes a Pakistani celebrity by accident."

cards.f: Karan Johar's directorial debut. Won 8 Filmfare awards. The basketball scene is not realistic.
cards.c: "Man realises he loved his best friend only after she married someone else. His daughter tries to fix this. She is eight."

cards.f: Changed how Indian films showed male friendship. Farhan Akhtar's directorial debut.
cards.c: "Three friends graduate and immediately stop agreeing on anything. One falls for a divorcee twice his age. The others are very judgy about this."

cards.f: The original was shot in 70mm widescreen. Played at one cinema in Mumbai for over five years.
cards.c: "Handicapped man hires two petty criminals to avenge his entire family. The criminals are not prepared for this job."

cards.f: Sanjay Dutt learned to play a doctor by shadowing real doctors at a Mumbai hospital for a month.
cards.c: "Gangster pretends to be a doctor to impress his parents. Accidentally becomes a better doctor than the actual doctors."

cards.f: The film sparked real student protests in India. Life imitated art in ways the filmmakers did not expect.
cards.c: "Group of friends make a documentary about freedom fighters and slowly become them. The government does not appreciate this."

cards.f: Aamir Khan directed this. The child actor Darsheel Safary was genuinely dyslexic.
cards.c: "Eight-year-old is sent to boarding school for being difficult. New teacher is the only adult who notices he is struggling. Parents needed a moment."

cards.f: Kangana Ranaut won the National Award for this. The entire film cost less than most Bollywood item numbers.
cards.c: "Woman gets dumped before her honeymoon. Goes on the honeymoon alone. Returns a completely different person. Ex-fiancé notices."

cards.f: Aamir Khan's highest-grossing film at the time. The nude poster caused significant controversy.
cards.c: "Alien loses his remote control in Rajasthan. Spends the film asking questions about religion that nobody wants to answer."

cards.f: Filmed entirely in Spain. Every extreme sport shown was done by the actors themselves.
cards.c: "Three friends on a road trip across Spain. Two of them love the same woman. Everyone learns something. Mostly about themselves."

cards.f: Karan Johar's most controversial film. Addressed marital unhappiness in Indian cinema for the first time at scale.
cards.c: "Two unhappily married people fall in love. Both spouses find out. Karan Johar explores consequences for once."

cards.f: Shahid Kapoor and Kareena were dating during filming and broke up shortly after. The chemistry was real. So was the breakup.
cards.c: "Man tries to get off a train. Woman won't let him. Not because she cares — she just won't stop talking."

cards.f: Flopped on release. Now considered the greatest Bollywood comedy ever made. It just needed time.
cards.c: "Two broke men both try to marry the same heiress. Neither has a plan. Both plans fail constantly."

cards.f: One of the most quoted Bollywood comedies ever. The trio chemistry was entirely improvised on set.
cards.c: "Three failures receive a wrong number call about a kidnapping ransom. Decide to keep the money. This goes poorly."

cards.f: Aamir Khan gained 25 kg and then lost it all for different phases of filming. True story of the Phogat sisters.
cards.c: "Father forces daughters to wrestle. Daughters are furious. They win Olympic gold. Father was right apparently."

cards.f: Chaiyya Chaiyya was shot on a moving train roof. AR Rahman. One take.
cards.c: "Journalist falls for a woman who is about to blow something up. He knows this. He escalates anyway."

cards.f: Shah Rukh Khan's Om Shanti Om was a tribute to the 70s Bollywood era. 30+ stars appeared in the title song.
cards.c: "Nobody man dies. Comes back with better cheekbones and a film career. Uses both to settle a score."

cards.f: Shot in Chanderi, a real town in MP. Locals were briefed on the film's plot before production began.
cards.c: "Men in a small town are vanishing during the festival. They write a polite note on their doors asking her to come back tomorrow."

cards.f: Imran Khan's debut. A horse played a significant supporting role. Horse's performance was uncontested.
cards.c: "Boy and girl are just friends. Insist on this for two hours. Nobody believes them. They eventually stop believing themselves."

cards.f: Priyadarshan remake of his own Malayalam film. Vidya Balan's performance in the climax is studied in acting schools.
cards.c: "Family moves into a haunted mansion. Calls a psychiatrist instead of a priest. Psychiatrist makes things significantly worse."

cards.f: Zoya Akhtar made the dog the narrator. Aamir Khan voiced the dog. Structural decision of the decade.
cards.c: "Rich family goes on a cruise to fix their failing marriages. The dog is the only one being honest."

cards.f: Irrfan Khan and Nimrat Kaur never share a scene. The film runs entirely on that tension.
cards.c: "Wrong delivery address starts a correspondence between two strangers. They write letters. They never meet. Both are fine with this. Both are not fine with this."

cards.f: Farhan Akhtar lost significant weight for this role. The twist was spoiled in the trailer. Nobody noticed until they watched it.
cards.c: "Man receives motivational calls every morning. Calls are working great. Caller is him."

cards.f: Sriram Raghavan based this on a French short film. The ending deliberately refuses to confirm what really happened.
cards.c: "Man pretends to have a disability. Witnesses something he shouldn't. Cannot reveal what he saw without revealing the lie. Situation worsens weekly."

cards.f: Anurag Kashyap's reimagining of Devdas. The only version where Devdas is sympathetic for about 20 minutes.
cards.c: "Man is given unconditional love by two women. Responds by making terrible decisions for two hours. Gets a redemption arc he did not earn."

cards.f: Kangana Ranaut stole the film from a lead who had twice her screen time. Critics could not explain how.
cards.c: "Quiet doctor falls for chaotic woman. Everyone tells him she is wrong for him. They marry. Everyone was correct."

cards.f: Ranbir Kapoor learned to actually play guitar for this role. AR Rahman scored it. The grief is real.
cards.c: "Young man wants to be a rockstar but is too happy. Is told he needs to suffer. Suffers enormously. Becomes a rockstar."

cards.f: Ranbir Kapoor studied Charlie Chaplin for six months. Priyanka Chopra's performance was entirely physical.
cards.c: "Man cannot speak or hear. Still the most charismatic person in every room he enters. Everyone else is embarrassed by this."

cards.f: Farhan Akhtar ran 13km a day for 13 months. His body fat dropped to 4%. Real Milkha Singh cried watching it.
cards.c: "Partition survivor becomes India's fastest man. Loses one race. Never forgives himself. The nation forgave him immediately."

cards.f: Filmed entirely on location in Kolkata during Durga Puja. The city becomes the co-star.
cards.c: "Pregnant woman searches for her missing husband in a city that has never heard of him. She is very determined. The city will regret this."

cards.f: Set in 1953. Vikramaditya Motwane based it on an O. Henry short story. Sonakshi Sinha watches a tree for 40 minutes of screen time.
cards.c: "Man cons a zamindaar's family. Falls genuinely in love with the daughter. Leaves anyway. She watches a tree and waits."

cards.f: Richa Chadha and Vicky Kaushal's breakthrough. Won two prizes at Cannes. Shot on a Rs 4 crore budget.
cards.c: "Two separate grief stories in Varanasi. They never intersect. The Ganga connects them anyway."

cards.f: Imtiaz Ali told the same love story twice across two timelines. One of them learns from the other. Barely.
cards.c: "Man celebrates his breakup. Meets an old man who tells him a love story. Realises the story is a warning. Does not heed it."

cards.f: Hrithik Roshan disguised as the Queen of England in the opening scene. Nobody questioned the casting.
cards.c: "Cop spends the entire film admiring the man he is supposed to arrest. The man is flattered. This is the dynamic."

cards.f: Anushka Sharma produced and starred. The film went to places Bollywood rarely visited. Critics were unsettled.
cards.c: "Couple on a road trip witnesses something they should not have. They intervene. They are now being hunted. Road trip ends differently than planned."

cards.f: Rishi Kapoor played a 90-year-old. The prosthetics took six hours to apply every day.
cards.c: "Dysfunctional family reunites because grandfather is dying. Every family secret surfaces in order. Grandfather watches."

cards.f: Vishal Bhardwaj set Hamlet in Kashmir during the 1990s conflict. The adaptation is scene-for-scene faithful.
cards.c: "Kashmir. Missing father. Suspicious uncle. Mother who may know more than she says. Shakespeare did not set it here but he could have."

cards.f: John Abraham produced. The film opened a national conversation about sperm donation. Urologist calls were up.
cards.c: "Man is recruited to donate sperm for a fertility clinic. Is very good at this. Wife finds out later. The conversation is difficult."

cards.f: Irrfan Khan won the National Award. The real Paan Singh Tomar held 7 national records. The sports federation lost his paperwork.
cards.c: "National champion athlete becomes a bandit because the sports federation loses his paperwork. The government created this problem."

cards.f: Anushka Sharma's debut. SRK changed his entire body language for the alter-ego Raj. Wife does not notice. This says something.
cards.c: "Wife does not recognise her own husband when he changes his hairstyle. Husband uses this to date his own wife. It works for longer than it should."

cards.f: Vikramaditya Motwane's debut. Won best film at multiple festivals. The father character became a reference point for cinematic villainy.
cards.c: "Teenager expelled from boarding school is forced to work in his father's factory. Father is the actual villain of this film."

cards.f: Made on a tiny budget. Became one of the highest-grossing comedies of the year. Proof that chaos scales.
cards.c: "Four idiots learn the location of hidden money. Race every other idiot in India to reach it first. Nobody is qualified for this."

cards.f: Two men faking a relationship for a flat was considered edgy in 2008. The flat was very nice.
cards.c: "Two men tell one lie to rent a flat. The lie works so well it becomes their entire personality for six months."

cards.f: Based on real caste atrocity cases in UP. The director spent a year researching before writing a single scene.
cards.c: "IPS officer posted to rural UP discovers a caste atrocity being covered up. His colleagues are the ones covering it up."

cards.f: Mani Ratnam was attacked at his home after release. AR Rahman's Bollywood debut. Banned in several states.
cards.c: "Hindu-Muslim couple in Bombay. Their city catches fire. A film about love that nobody wanted to make."

cards.f: Ram Gopal Varma. Manoj Bajpayee's breakthrough. Essentially invented the Mumbai crime film as a genre.
cards.c: "Innocent man arrives in Bombay. Gets absorbed into the underworld. By the end he is the underworld."

cards.f: Five and a half hours. Three generations. Anurag Kashyap shot it over 62 days without a single day off.
cards.c: "Three generations of one family spend 70 years trying to kill one other family. Nobody finishes the job. Everyone is also in love with someone inconvenient."

cards.f: Took 6 years to make. Shot in Pune, Maharashtra and Iceland. The goddess is practical effects, not CGI.
cards.c: "Man visits a cursed goddess every monsoon to steal from her. Goes well for decades. Then takes his son along."

cards.f: Nawazuddin Siddiqui based his character on the real Raman Raghav who terrorised Bombay in the 1960s.
cards.c: "Serial killer meets a cop who might be worse than him. They are fascinated by each other. This is not a buddy film."

cards.f: Vishal Bhardwaj's second Shakespeare adaptation. The dialect is from western UP. Ajay Devgn studied it for months.
cards.c: "Othello but in UP gang politics and dialect. A sari replaces the handkerchief. Ends the same way."

cards.f: Ranbir Kapoor gained 10 kg for this role. The film runs 3.5 hours. The ending sets up a sequel nobody agreed to.
cards.c: "Man loves his father. Father does not notice. Man escalates."

cards.f: Sanjay Leela Bhansali was attacked on set. The film was banned in four states before release.
cards.c: "King spends an entire war trying to see one woman's face. She has thoughts about this."

cards.f: Anurag Kashyap shelved this film for four years due to political pressure. Piyush Mishra wrote the lyrics while on set.
cards.c: "Law student arrives in Rajasthan and is absorbed into a royalist political movement. Realises too late what he has joined."

cards.f: Anurag Kashyap's most underrated film. Every adult in the frame is failing the child. That is the point.
cards.c: "Child goes missing. Father, ex-husband, stepfather, and the cops all suspect each other. Nobody is looking for the child."

cards.f: Naseeruddin Shah and Anupam Kher. 104 minutes. No filler. Still the most efficient Bollywood thriller made.
cards.c: "Ordinary man calls the police commissioner with a bomb threat. His reasons are not what anyone expects."

cards.f: Vishal Bhardwaj's first Shakespeare adaptation. The witches are corrupt cops who also predict the future. It works.
cards.c: "Macbeth but in the Mumbai underworld. The witches are corrupt cops. Everything else is identical."

cards.f: Sudhir Mishra's most personal film. The Emergency-era politics are accurate. The heartbreak is worse.
cards.c: "Three friends. Emergency-era India. One becomes a revolutionary. One becomes the establishment. One watches both and cannot choose."

cards.f: Nagesh Kukunoor directed. Ayesha Takia and Gul Panag. Shot in Rajasthan and Himachal. Two women who should not meet, must.
cards.c: "Two women. One's husband sentenced to death for allegedly killing the other's. One must travel to get the other to forgive."

cards.f: True story of advocate Shahid Azmi, who was assassinated in 2010. Rajkummar Rao won the National Award.
cards.c: "Criminal lawyer defends the accused while becoming an accused himself. True story. The irony was not lost on him."

cards.f: India's Oscar entry in 2017. A government clerk tries to conduct a fair election in a Naxal-controlled area. Systems resist.
cards.c: "Government clerk is sent to conduct an election in a conflict zone. He insists on doing it properly. Everyone finds this inconvenient."

cards.f: Meghna Gulzar directed. Based on the Aarushi Talwar case. Irrfan Khan plays the CBI investigator. Three versions of the truth, none satisfying.
cards.c: "A girl is found dead. Three agencies investigate. Each arrives at a different truth. The parents are convicted on the least convincing one."

cards.f: Anurag Kashyap's most personal film about caste, love, and boxing. The final match sequence runs 15 uncut minutes.
cards.c: "Boxer falls for his coach's niece. Coach is a powerful upper-caste man. Love and ambition both require the same sacrifice."

cards.f: Shakun Batra. Deepika Padukone. The anxiety is physical. The apartment is the most expensive character in the film.
cards.c: "Woman in a relationship starts another relationship. Both men are connected. The web tightens until someone drowns. Possibly literally."

cards.f: Abhishek Chaubey directed. Sushant Singh Rajput. Shot in the Chambal ravines with real dacoits as extras.
cards.c: "Dacoits in the Chambal ravines protect a girl the police also want. Honour is debated by men with rifles. The landscape does not care."
